content:
As experts in laser cleaning technology, we present data on the effectiveness of laser ablation for removing contaminants from {material} substrates. Laser cleaning offers precise, non-contact removal of oxides, coatings, and residues in industries like aerospace and automotive. Metrics such as ablation depth (μm) and surface roughness (Ra) post-cleaning are critical. Note that effectiveness can vary slightly due to substrate porosity or contaminant bonding—e.g., rust on steel may require higher fluence (J/cm²). Below is the JSON chart data for laser cleaning performance on {material}.

```json
{
  "labels": ["Ablation Depth (μm)", "Surface Roughness (Ra)", "Cleaning Efficiency (%)", "Fluence Required (J/cm²)"],
  "values": [12.5, 0.8, 92.3, 5.2]
}
```
